name: ridenum
title: ridenum

commands.name: ridenum
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# ridenum -h
 
 .______       __   _______         _______ .__   __.  __    __  .___  ___.
 |   _  \     |  | |       \       |   ____||  \ |  | |  |  |  | |   \/   |
 |  |_)  |    |  | |  .--.  |      |  |__   |   \|  | |  |  |  | |  \  /  |
 |      /     |  | |  |  |  |      |   __|  |  . `  | |  |  |  | |  |\/|  |
 |  |\  \----.|  | |  '--'  |      |  |____ |  |\   | |  `--'  | |  |  |  |
 | _| `._____||__| |_______/  _____|_______||__| \__|  \______/  |__|  |__|
                           |______|
 
 Written by: David Kennedy (ReL1K)
 Company: https://www.trustedsec.com
 Twitter: @TrustedSec
 Twitter: @HackingDave
 
 Rid Enum is a RID cycling attack that attempts to enumerate user accounts through
 null sessions and the SID to RID enum. If you specify a password file, it will
 automatically attempt to brute force the user accounts when its finished enumerating.
 
 - RIDENUM is open source and uses all standard python libraries minus python-pexpect. -
 
 You can also specify an already dumped username file, it needs to be in the DOMAINNAME\\USERNAME
 format.
 
 Example: ./ridenum.py 192.168.1.50 500 50000 /root/dict.txt /root/user.txt
 
 Usage: ./ridenum.py <server_ip> <start_rid> <end_rid> <optional_username> <optional_password> <optional_password_file> <optional_username_filename>

examples.command:
ridenum 192.168.1.236 500 50000 /tmp/passes.txt
[*] Attempting lsaquery first...This will enumerate the base domain SID
[*] Successfully enumerated base domain SID.. Moving on to extract via RID
[*] Enumerating user accounts.. This could take a little while.

apt_metadata.description:
Null session RID cycle attack tool
Rid Enum is a RID cycling attack that attempts to enumerate user accounts
through null sessions and the SID to RID enum. If you specify a password file,
it will automatically attempt to brute force the user accounts when its
finished enumerating.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ridenum because ### ridenum.

When you need a concrete invocation, run `./ridenum.py <server_ip> <start_rid> <end_rid> <optional_username> <optional_password> <optional_password_file> <optional_username_filename>` as documented upstream for ridenum.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ridenum with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ridenum --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install ridenum. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `./ridenum.py <server_ip> <start_rid> <end_rid> <optional_username> <optional_password> <optional_password_file> <optional_username_filename>`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### ridenum.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install ridenum
Install with: sudo apt install ridenum
